posts.title: Recent Developments in LLM Architectures: KV Sharing, mHC, and Compressed Attention
posts.author: Sebastian Raschka
posts.summary: From Gemma 4 to DeepSeek V4, How New Open-Weight LLMs Are Reducing Long-Context Costs
posts.highlights: This post covers recent advances in LLM architectures aimed at reducing memory and compute costs for long-context processing, including KV sharing, multi-head caching (mHC), and compressed attention mechanisms. Key examples include Gemma 4's and DeepSeek V4's approaches to efficient attention, which enable handling longer sequences without proportional resource increases.
posts.worthReading: For practitioners and researchers working with LLMs, this article provides a concise overview of cutting-edge techniques that address the scalability bottleneck of long-context models, offering practical insights into how open-weight models are evolving.

posts.title: Latest open artifacts (#21): Open model bonanza! Gemma 4, DeepSeek V4, Kimi K2.6, MiMo 2.5, GLM-5.1 & others. On CAISI's V4 assessment.
posts.author: Nathan Lambert
posts.summary: An eventful month with one flagship release after another
posts.highlights: The post reviews a wave of major open model releases including Gemma 4, DeepSeek V4, Kimi K2.6, MiMo 2.5, and GLM-5.1, highlighting the rapid pace of innovation in open AI. It focuses on CAISI's V4 assessment, providing a comparative analysis of performance and capabilities across these models.
posts.worthReading: If you follow open-source AI, this post offers a concise yet comprehensive snapshot of the latest model landscape, saving you hours of individual paper reviews.
